symptoms.symptom_name: Intense pruritus with continuous excoriation and pinpoint bleeding

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excoriation of pruritic acne lesions produces the raw bleeding surfaces seen.

symptoms.symptom_name: Comedones and seborrhoea on face and upper trunk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Comedonal origin defines acne and argues against impetigo/eczema herpeticum as primary.
Comedonal origin does not exclude secondary infection.
No honey crusts or fever reduces probability of established impetigo/cellulitis.
Afebrile with no punched-out monomorphic erosions argues strongly against eczema herpeticum.
Their presence would mandate antiviral therapy; absent on inspection here.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Acne and excoriation are clinical; no routine bloods needed without systemic features.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bacterial swab MC&S if superinfection suspected
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Guide antibiotics if honey crusts, purulence or spreading warmth develop.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral swab/PCR for HSV if punched-out erosions appear
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To confirm/exclude eczema herpeticum if morphology changes.
